title: Gate and repair activity painter
goal: Make the Herdr Integration Gate / Repair pane show live daemon-owned gate and repair command activity when commands are running, while retaining summary/status output when no command is active.
acceptance: The Integration Gate / Repair Herdr pane can render live daemon-owned gate or repair command activity while a command is running and falls back to the existing daemon feed/status summary otherwise.
Gate/repair painter input comes only from daemon-owned gate/shell artifacts or status feed data; it never reads Herdr pane scrollback or UI state as workflow evidence.
The gate/shell correctness path is unchanged: command exit status, stdout/stderr artifacts, typed gate results, repair decisions, verification, and handoff readiness remain daemon-owned.
Renderer failures are non-fatal visibility incidents and do not change gate result, repair policy, run status, slice status, merge, or handoff behavior.
Live command output is rate-limited or compacted so verbose gates remain readable without losing the underlying artifacts.
Tests cover both active command rendering and idle summary rendering without requiring Herdr for the normal cargo test suite.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make Herdr required for gate or repair execution.
The implementation would parse terminal scrollback or pane text as gate/repair correctness evidence.
The implementation would alter verification, repair, merge, or handoff decisions based on painter output.
The implementation would remove or weaken existing daemon-owned gate/shell result artifacts.
